title: Multiple outputs using a single line

content.problemStatement:
You can also have multiple outputs together in a single "print" command. \
It can be done using the ```kotlin"$"``` symbol.
```kotlin"$"``` references a variable or expression in a string template, don't worry about it now we will learn about that in upcoming modules.

So, for example,
```kotlin
print(2)
println()
print(3)
```
and
```kotlin
print("${2}\n${3}") 

Here "\n" is used for new line it works same as "println" and "${}" we will learn more about this in coming modules, for now, just remember it as parentheses which can be useful to work on integers, some expressions, conditions, etc.
```
are equivalent.

Output:
```kotlin
2
3
```

And there is also another way of printing output in new line
```kotlin
print("" + 2 + "\n" + 3)  

You can also perform operations on this
print("" + (2+2) + "\n" +(2+1))
```

### Task
Write a program which does the following
- Output: 3 + 4
- Output: 2 + 1 in a separate line using the syntax suggested above.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ â€” the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
